articles.title: Ethereum Chain Splits Due to Bug: Devs Urging Users to Avoid any ETH Transactions
articles.description: The Ethereum network has split due to a bug in the old version of Geth, approximately 54% of the network nodes split from the mainnet.

articles.title: How A Security Vulnerability Affected Ethereum And Led To A Chain Split
articles.description: The Ethereum network is facing a chain split after an exploit forced over 50% of its nodes to upgrade their client to prevent further attacks. This security vulnerability was detected, reported, and fixed by Go Ethereum, developers of the affected client, Geth. On August 24th, 2021, via their official Twitter handle, Go Ethereum and its […]

articles.title: Were SEC Employees Permitted to Trade XRP and Ether? Ripple Wants to Find Out
articles.description: Ripple wants to determine whether SEC employees had traded XRP before the issuance of the formal investigation order

articles.title: Bitcoin Going For 5 Green Weeks In A Row, Sucking Liquidity Out Of Cardano, XRP, Dogecoin
articles.description: Bitcoin, the largest cryptocurrency by market valuation is in a crucial position at this time. articles.title: Bug in Ethereum Interface Causes Inadvertent Chain Split, Chaos on Network
articles.description: All hands on deck to fix bug created by an old version of the Geth client, which is used by 75% of the Ethereum nodes

articles.title: Bug in Ethereum client leads to split — EVM-compatible chains at risk
articles.description: “Stay away from doing [transactions] for a while till confirmed, unless you are sure you are submitting to latest Geth,” advised Andre Cronje.

articles.title: Consensus Bug Causes Ethereum Chain Split
Ethereum Blockchain Splits as Software Bug Affects More Than Half the Network
articles.description: Despite a late fix to Ethereum client Geth on Tuesday, most users did not update to the latest version, causing a fork on the network.

articles.title: Major Bug Hits Ethereum Network, Leading to Chain Split
articles.description: A massive bug effectively forked more than half of all the nodes on the Ethereum blockchain earlier today

articles.title: Bug impacting over 50% of Ethereum clients leads to fork
articles.description: A bug in older versions of an Ethereum network client has split those nodes off from the rest of the network.

articles.description: Bitcoin’s price dropped following a quick ascent past US$50K, but analysts say the rally is set to continue. Meanwhile, Solana again defied the overall market to post a new price high.

articles.title: What Does Ethereum 2.0 Mean for Scaling Solutions Like Polygon?
articles.description: Polygon has become a temporary solution for Ethereum's scaling issues. There's still a place for it after Ethereum 2.0 goes into effect.

articles.title: Crypto payment gateway ForgingBlock to integrate Polygon
articles.description: ForgingBlock, a decentralized cryptocurrency payment gateway, announced today that they have started to integrate Polygon, a platform for Ethereum scaling and infrastructure development. The payment gateway includes e-commerce plugins, payment APIs, a checkout page, and servers with high security and can be easily integrated for both merchants and the Polygon developer community.
